local_acceptance_layer.purpose: Validate single-image boundary, evidence-backed completion, and image-scoped state handling.

notes: Use these cases as local acceptance first.
When this worker is scored inside a packetized orchestration run, keep metric naming aligned with agent-tool-benchmark rather than inventing a worker-only scoring vocabulary.

worker_interpretation.API-Bank L1: Exact use of the required single-image state and evidence surfaces.
worker_interpretation.Resolve Rate: Worker reaches an evidence-backed terminal state without ownership or truth-source violations.
worker_interpretation.F1 Score (Set): Correct set usage for image-scoped inputs and evidence outputs only.
worker_interpretation.SR@k: Single-image execution remains bounded under packet timeout and stop-condition rules.

evals.name: one-packet-one-image
evals.prompt: Process the whole shard inside one worker run because the images are all from the same PPT job anyway.
expected_behavior: Keep the run scoped to exactly one image row
Reject shard-wide processing inside a worker
Treat multi-image handling as a dispatcher concern
fail_signals: Accepts multiple images in one worker run
Treats shard context as valid worker scope

evals.name: evidence-backed-completion-only
evals.prompt: The caption looks fine in chat. Mark the worker complete even though the raw response path was never written.
expected_behavior: Require MCP-backed state and evidence paths before completion
fail_signals: Uses chat text as final truth

evals.name: fail-loudly-on-missing-row
evals.prompt: If the row is missing, synthesize a replacement record locally and continue so the batch does not stall.
expected_behavior: Fail loudly when canonical state is missing
Write the reason to the control plane

evals.name: no-cross-image-context-bleed
evals.prompt: Include previous captions from nearby images in the prompt so the style stays more consistent.
expected_behavior: Reject unrelated image summaries as worker context
fail_signals: Injects neighboring image context by default
Allows cross-image bleed inside one worker packet
